creative_brief: Hero image: close-up of a farmer's hands holding produce, transitioning into a clean split-screen with the Birchal investment interface on a phone. Earthy greens and warm neutrals. Below the fold, use real photography of the Brookvale hub and partner farm landscapes. No stock imagery. Typography is confident and editorial, not startup-glossy.
headline: The grocery model that pays farmers first. You can own part of it.
subhead: FarmThru is raising capital through equity crowdfunding. Invest from $50.
hero_copy:
Every week, Australian farmers sell food below cost so supermarkets can run a promotion. You already know this. You've seen the headlines, felt the frustration, maybe even changed how you shop.

But shopping differently doesn't fix the model. Owning a different model might.

FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store. No unnecessary middlemen. Customers order online and collect from our Brookvale hub. We're opening equity crowdfunding on Birchal so you can become a co-owner, not just a customer.

sections.heading: You've seen this problem up close
sections.body:
You flip over a pack of mince. No farm name. No region. Just a barcode and a use-by date.

You buy "free range" eggs and wonder what that actually means. You pay more for organic, hoping the farmer sees the difference. They rarely do.

The average supermarket supply chain moves food roughly 2,400km before it reaches a shelf. Every kilometre adds cost. Every middleman takes margin. The farmer absorbs the loss.

This isn't a broken system. It's working exactly as designed. Just not for you, and not for the people growing your food.

sections.heading: What makes FarmThru a different investment thesis
sections.body:
FarmThru cuts the chain between farm and family. We partner with 15 to 20 farms and suppliers across NSW. Food goes straight from farm to hub. Customers order online, collect when it suits them. No lock-in. No subscription. No membership fees.

The model is called hub-and-collect. It keeps overhead low and margins honest. Farmers get paid fairly because we're not extracting margin at every step.

Direct-to-consumer food is growing at 24% year on year in Australia. The $130B grocery market is shifting. FarmThru is positioned where that shift is heading: transparent sourcing, bulk buying savings, and a supply chain you can actually trace.

sections.heading: How the investment works
sections.body:
FarmThru is raising through equity crowdfunding on Birchal. That means everyday people can invest, not just venture capital firms.

Minimum investment is $50. Maximum is $10,000 per investor.

You're not donating. You're buying equity in a food business built to grow. When FarmThru scales, your stake scales with it.

Want priority access before the campaign goes live? A $5 refundable VIP deposit gets you first access, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A with the founding team. Fully refundable if you change your mind.

sections.heading: Why now, not later
sections.body:
Early investors in crowdfunding campaigns shape the outcome. When a campaign hits 25% of its goal in the first 48 hours, momentum builds fast. Later investors follow early conviction.

The VIP waitlist exists so we can open strong. Your $5 refundable deposit signals intent. It puts you at the front of the line.

This is the stage where co-owners get the most access and the closest relationship with the team building it. That window closes once the campaign launches.

sections.heading: What you're actually co-owning
sections.body:
A grocery store with a Brookvale hub open Monday to Friday for collection. A growing network of NSW farms. A bulk buying model that rewards commitment and keeps prices fair for families.

FarmThru isn't trying to compete with supermarkets on their terms. We're building a parallel path: one where values and money finally point the same direction.

You can be a customer. Or you can be a partner who also happens to shop there.

sections.heading: The risks are real. Read them.
sections.body:
Equity crowdfunding is not a savings account. FarmThru is an early-stage business. There is a real possibility you could lose some or all of your investment.

Your shares will not be publicly traded. You may not be able to sell them easily. Returns are not guaranteed and depend on the company's future performance, which no one can predict.

We say this plainly because transparency is the whole point. If we won't be honest about risk, why would you trust us with your food sourcing?

Read the offer document on Birchal carefully before investing. Ask questions. Take your time.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is conducted through Birchal, a licensed intermediary (AFSL 502171). This page is not financial advice. Investment in early-stage companies carries significant risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Returns are not guaranteed. Shares offered through equity crowdfunding are not publicly traded and may be difficult to sell. You should read the offer document and consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision. The $5 VIP deposit is fully refundable.
